steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Transitions. The first sentence states a principle: regionally popular sports at past Olympics were given only onetime showcases (unlike international sports). The second sentence gives two concrete examples — speed skating and glíma — that illustrate this principle. The transition must signal that the examples follow logically from the rule stated.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Granted,’: signals a concession or acknowledgment of a counterpoint — used when the writer wants to admit something that complicates the prior claim. The examples here confirm, not complicate, the principle. Wrong logical relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘However,’: signals contrast or contradiction with the preceding idea. The examples do not contradict the stated principle; they support it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Accordingly,’: signals that what follows is a consequence of or is consistent with what came before — exactly the relationship between a general rule and the specific examples that demonstrate it. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Moreover,’: signals addition of a new, separate point. The second sentence does not add an independent claim; it illustrates the first sentence’s claim with examples. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
